channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bought a Clif Bar? You could be paid as part of a class action settlement
item.description: A lawsuit against Clif Bar & Company alleged that Clif Bars and Clif Kid ZBars were unhealthy due to added sugars, despite being advertised with slogans like "Nutrition for Sustained Energy" and "No High Fructose Corn Syrup" on the product's packaging.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KTLA 5

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — With his major opponents imprisoned or left off the ballot, Tunisian President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning reelection on Sunday, five years after riding anti-establishment backlash to a first term. The North African country's Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: International fiesta fills New Mexico's sky with colorful hot air balloons
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — One of the most photographed events in the world is set to kick off Saturday with a mass ascension of color for the 52nd annual Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: For migrant women who land in Colorado looking for jobs, a common answer emerges: No
item.description: As record numbers of South Americans come to the U.S. seeking better economic opportunities, many are landing in communities that are unprepared for them — and sometimes outright hostile.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Earth's 2nd moon has arrived: What you need to know
item.description: A second moon has officially entered Earth's orbit—sort of. Although it's being called a "mini-moon," it's actually an asteroid named 2024 PT5. The asteroid has been temporarily captured by Earth's gravitational pull. The asteroid will remain in Earth's orbit from now until Nov.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 14 dogs from South Carolina arrive in Rhode Island after Hurricane Helene
item.description: "We are hoping that these dogs get adopted quickly so that we have some space so we can call them again and try to help them some more because they are dealing with so much," Potter League CEO Brad Shear said.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles DA George Gascon will review new evidence in Menendez brothers murder case
item.description: New evidence was presented to the Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office that needs to be reviewed in the Menendez brothers case, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New video shows fallout from wild, violent street takeovers in Cleveland
item.description: Dozens of cars and huge crowds last weekend tied up traffic in Cleveland on the Innerbelt Bridge and on streets all over the city.
item.dc:creator: Ed Gallek, NewsNation

item.title: East Tennessee hospital employee recounts rooftop rescue during flood
item.description: "I think when you start looking at the data and seeing the magnitude of what happened, there's no predictability about that," said Harrah.
item.dc:creator: Alex Libby, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout
item.description: "Phillip was mum about his second winning ticket because he couldn't find it," according to the lottery's website.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 3 ex-officers convicted in fatal beating of Tyre Nichols
item.description: Former Memphis police officers Tadarrius Bean, Demetrius Haley and Justin Smith were convicted Thursday in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but cleared of the most serious charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RSV, COVID, flu shots: Who should get vaccinated, according to the CDC
item.description: "The vaccines have arrived. And so if you if you do one thing this week or this weekend, try to take a minute to schedule time to go get you and your family, your vaccines," Shah said.
item.dc:creator: Marlo Lacen, NewsNation

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon is convicted of threatening former Speaker McCarthy
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man was convicted Wednesday of threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy after becoming upset that the government had not shot down a Chinese spy balloon that floated over his home city.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CNN launches digital paywall for the first time
item.description: CNN, one of the most visited news sites in the world, announced Tuesday that visitors will soon have to pay to view the website. CNN.com will implement a paywall, asking visitors to pay $3.99 a month or a discounted rate of $29.99 a year to access its news content.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: 5 Chinese nationals charged with covering up midnight visit to Michigan military site
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — U.S. authorities charged five Chinese nationals with lying and trying to cover their tracks, more than a year after they were confronted in the dark near a remote Michigan military site where thousands of people had gathered for summer drills.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wyoming town told to 'ready' for evacuation as wildfire spreads 32,000 acres
item.description: The town of Dayton, Wyoming, has been put on a "ready to evacuate" status as a massive wildfire continues to spread across the Bighorn National Forest just six miles away.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Pilot flying Helene rescue missions in NC ordered out, threatened with arrest
item.description: The fire official told him to tell any other pilots he knew they would also be arrested if they came back.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, Fox 5

item.title: Port strike may hike prices, cause shortages of certain goods: What to know
item.description: U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, KRON4

item.title: Tile's Back With Trackers That One-Up AirTags
item.description: Remember Tile? They were one of the first Bluetooth tracking tags, and now they have four new products. The company—fresh off its Life360 acquisition—hopes to stand out in a world of AirTags and Android Trackers with unique features.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, ABC 4

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: At least 64 dead and millions without power after Helene's deadly march across the Southeast
item.description: PERRY, Fla. (AP) — Massive rains from powerful Hurricane Helene left people stranded, without shelter and awaiting rescue Saturday, as the cleanup began from a tempest that killed at least 64 people, caused widespread destruction across the U.S. Southeast and left millions without power.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN SMITH, KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, WGN 9
